brief_sections.content: COLLATZ-NATIVE-MATH is a native structural reading of Collatz dynamics. It studies the structures generated by the transformation itself before translating them into proof-oriented language.
brief_sections.title: What this project is

brief_sections.content: It is not a proof of Collatz. It is not a claim that Collatz is solved. It is not a theorem layer. It is not a global closure claim. It is not a global invariant claim.
brief_sections.title: What this project is not

brief_sections.content: What is Collatz before it becomes a conjecture?
brief_sections.title: Central question

brief_sections.content: Native language means describing Collatz through the structures produced by its own rule: expansion, discharge, debt, shadow, regeneration, compensation, closure, and obstruction.
brief_sections.title: What native language means here

brief_sections.content: The bounded artifacts show dangerous native sentence forms, danger-release behavior, dangerous stability candidates, and invariant candidates. No obstruction-relevant invariant candidate is detected in the current bounded maps.
brief_sections.title: What the bounded artifacts currently show

brief_sections.content: The artifacts are bounded and descriptive. Bounded absence of obstruction does not imply global impossibility. Native grammar does not equal theorem. The termination question remains a later classical translation endpoint.
brief_sections.title: Why this is not a proof

brief_sections.content: The repository has a passing native artifact consistency audit, a native language index, and a consolidated native audit report.
brief_sections.title: Current repository integrity

brief_sections.content: The next work is to make the public brief easier to read, then continue native-language refinement without collapsing back into solution-first pressure.
brief_sections.title: Where the work continues

central_question: What is Collatz before it becomes a conjecture?
one_sentence_summary: COLLATZ-NATIVE-MATH studies Collatz as a native dynamical language before treating it as a conjecture to solve.
primary_position: A native structural reading of the Collatz dynamics.
public_claims_allowed: A native structural reading of the Collatz dynamics.
The project starts from native description, not proof.
The repository currently has a passing consistency audit.
No obstruction-relevant invariant candidate is detected in the current bounded maps.
public_claims_forbidden: A proof of Collatz is provided.
short_public_description: This project does not begin by trying to prove Collatz. It begins by asking what Collatz generates in its own structural language.
